meta.title: Kitchen Display System for Hospitality — Kitchen Monitor & Pick-Up Screen | Gastro Master
meta.description: Kitchen display system from Gastro Master: pick-up screen for guests and kitchen monitor for the team. All orders in real time, colour-coded by priority, no paper chaos. For fast food restaurants, delivery services, cafes and bakeries.

hero.headline: Display System — Kitchen and Counter Always in the Picture
hero.subline: Two displays, one goal: full visibility. The pick-up screen shows collection customers their order number in real time, the kitchen monitor prioritises all open orders by time and category. No slips, no tickets, no forgotten orders.

problem.title: The Problem with High Order Volume
points: During rush hour, the kitchen loses track — tickets pile up, special requests get lost, guests wait unnecessarily long.
Paper tickets blow away, get damp, fall apart — and nobody knows which order needs to be ready first.
Collection customers constantly ask whether their order is ready — staff have to keep checking instead of cooking or serving.
Without clear prioritisation, 20-minute dishes end up next to 3-minute snacks — everything finishes at once or not at all.

solution.title: The Solution: Display System
points: The kitchen monitor immediately shows every order with table number, items, special requests and time — colour-coded by product category.
The pick-up screen calls collection customers by order number. Guests check themselves — staff no longer need to call out or search.
Prioritisation by order time: oldest order at the top, red highlight when a delay is looming. No dish ever gets forgotten again.
Auto-refresh in real time. Every new order from the POS, webshop, app or QR code table system appears instantly — no button press needed.

features.headline: Everything You Need for a Kitchen and Counter That Miss Nothing
features.sub: Six features that replace paper tickets and turn chaos into choreography.

items.title: Kitchen Monitor
items.description: All open orders at a glance — with table number, items, special requests, time and status. Mark as done with a single tap.

items.title: Pick-Up Screen for Guests
items.description: Large display in the collection area shows order numbers — In Progress vs. Ready to Collect. Guests see for themselves when it's their turn.

items.title: Colour-Coded Categories
items.description: Pizza red, salad green, drinks blue — every product category has its colour. Individual stations see at a glance what's needed from them.

items.title: Time-Based Prioritisation
items.description: Oldest order first. Automatic colour warning when an order has been open too long — no lost dish, no annoyed guest.

items.title: Auto-Refresh & Live Sync
items.description: Every order from the POS, webshop, app, delivery service or QR code table system lands instantly on screen. No delay, no manual refresh needed.

items.title: Counter, Freestanding & Outdoor
items.description: Three form factors for every location — countertop stand, freestanding display or weatherproof outdoor model for drive-throughs and beer gardens.

useCases.headline: How Restaurateurs Use the Display System
useCases.sub: Three real scenarios from our customer base — from fast food to the city bakery with drive-through.

items.title: Fast Food Restaurant with Delivery
items.story: A pizzeria in Hamburg handling collection, delivery and dine-in processes 60 orders per hour at peak times. Since introducing the kitchen monitor, 20% more orders are produced per day — with the same kitchen team.

items.title: Bakery with Counter Collection
items.story: A bakery chain in Cologne uses pick-up screens in the collection area. Customers order via app, collect during their lunch break and see their number on the display — queues at the counter have shrunk by 40%.

items.title: Food Court with Multiple Stations
items.story: An operator of several food concepts in a Munich shopping centre installed outdoor monitors in front of every stand. Guests see at a glance where their order is — staff enquiries per shift dropped from 80 to under 15.

stats.label: more orders per shift with the same kitchen team
fewer queues in the collection area
no paper, no lost orders anymore

testimonial.quote: On Saturday evenings we produce 80 pizzas an hour. Without the kitchen monitor that would be unthinkable — before, we were constantly losing tickets or doubling up on orders. Now every order is in its colour with time and priority. My chefs love it, my guests wait less.
testimonial.author: Marco D.

pricing.headline: Pricing & Availability
pricing.note: POS add-on for restaurants, delivery services, bakeries and food courts. The price depends on form factor and number of screens — counter, freestanding or outdoor. In a free consultation we put together the right setup for your business and give you a fixed price including installation.

internalLinks.headline: Works Perfectly With
internalLinks.intro: The display system unfolds its full potential as part of the Gastro Master ecosystem — from order to handover to the guest.

items.title: POS System
items.description: The heart of it all. Every order from POS, webshop, app or QR code lands automatically on the kitchen monitor — with table number, items and special requests.

items.title: Restaurant Solution
items.description: Complete package of POS, table management, display system and QR ordering — specifically for restaurants, cafes and bars with high order volume.

faq.headline: Frequently Asked Questions about the Display System
faq.sub: Answers to the questions we receive most often from restaurants, delivery services, bakeries and food courts.

items.q: What does the display system cost for my business?
items.a: The price depends on the form factor (counter, freestanding, outdoor) and the number of screens. We don't offer the display system as a list price — instead, after a free consultation we put together a specific quote. In many businesses the setup pays for itself within 3–6 months through time and labour savings.

items.q: What's the difference between the pick-up screen and the kitchen monitor?
items.a: The kitchen monitor hangs in the kitchen or at the counter and shows the team all open orders with table number, items, special requests and time. The pick-up screen hangs in the guest area or at the collection counter and shows collection customers their order number and status — In Progress or Ready to Collect. Both screens can be used individually or in combination.

Three: counter (to place on a counter or bar, slim and compact), freestanding (floor-standing display, ideal for collection areas and food courts) and outdoor (weatherproof, for drive-throughs, beer gardens or terraces). We'll advise you on the right model for your business.

items.q: How are the screens connected to my POS system?
items.a: If you already use a Gastro Master POS system, the screens are immediately ready. Orders from POS, webshop, app, delivery service or QR code table system land automatically in real time on the kitchen monitor and pick-up screen. No separate setup, no third parties, no developers needed.

Every order receives a timestamp on arrival. On the kitchen monitor they appear sorted by age — oldest at the top. The longer an order stays open, the more strongly it's highlighted in colour (yellow, then red). The kitchen immediately sees which order is at risk and can intervene before a guest complains.

The colours are freely configurable — typically one colour per station: hot kitchen, cold kitchen, pizza, drinks, desserts, sides. Each station sees at a glance what's queued for them and can mark their items as done directly — without the rest of the kitchen having to cross-check.

items.q: Which businesses benefit most from the display system?
items.a: Any business with high order volume or multiple kitchen stations: fast food restaurants, pizzerias, burger joints, delivery services, food courts, bakeries with a lunch menu, cafes with takeaway, drive-throughs. Particularly effective from 50 orders per day or when there are multiple order channels running in parallel.

items.q: Does the pick-up screen replace personal contact with the guest?
items.a: No — it takes the pressure off. Guests check the screen themselves to see whether their order is ready. Staff no longer need to call out or search and have time for consultation, new orders or packing. The personal handover remains — it just becomes more efficient.

items.q: Can I add more screens later as my business grows?
items.a: Yes. You can add more kitchen monitors or pick-up screens at any time — for example when you open a second location, add a drive-through or set up a new station in the kitchen. All screens are centrally configurable in the backoffice.

items.q: What happens during an internet or power outage?
items.a: The screens have fail-safe protection. During a brief internet outage, the current order view continues, new orders are buffered in the POS and sync again as soon as the connection is restored. During a power outage, the display restarts automatically after recovery and reloads the last state from the POS.
